title: Middle East tensions and Iran negotiations impact markets and oil
summary: Stock markets react to talks between the US and Iran, while oil rises amid geopolitical uncertainty.

title: The rise of artificial intelligence boosts tech stocks
summary: Companies like Nvidia, Palantir, and Corning benefit from growing interest in AI, while Apple adjusts prices due to chip shortages.

title: SpaceX enters stock indices and issues a major bond
summary: Elon Musk's aerospace company is included in the Nasdaq 100 and other indices, while its stock rises after the IPO.

title: Risk warnings and investment advice amid market volatility
summary: Analysts point to warning signs not seen since 2000, while Warren Buffett recommends strategies for market downturns.

title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000 and UK regulator eases crypto rules
summary: The largest cryptocurrency loses ground as Strategy announces stock buyback and Ionic Digital seeks to list on Nasdaq.

title: Comcast announces spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky into two independent companies
summary: The media company splits to separate its content assets from its technology business, boosting the stock.

title: Rocket Lab acquires Iridium in historic $8 billion deal
summary: The merger creates a vertically integrated space giant, boosting shares of both companies.

title: European stocks trade mixed awaiting ECB and Middle East
summary: The IBEX loses 19,400 points while the DAX closes unchanged, in a session marked by caution.

title: Dow Jones hits new record and Alphabet joins the index
summary: The industrial average surpasses 52,000 points for the first time, while Alphabet shares rise strongly on its debut in the Dow.

title: Lagarde says ECB can raise rates without fear of financial tensions
summary: The President of the European Central Bank defends restrictive monetary policy without the need for protective measures.

title: US Supreme Court blocks firing of Fed Governor Lisa Cook
summary: The court rejects Trump's request to remove the official, protecting the independence of the central bank.

title: Yen falls to lowest level in 39 and a half years against the dollar
summary: The Japanese currency weakens to 162 yen per dollar, pressured by monetary policy divergence.

title: Nikkei continues to rise driven by gains on Wall Street
summary: The Japanese index opens with a gain of more than 700 points, continuing the positive trend in US markets.

title: Paraguay beats Germany on penalties and Brazil comes back against Japan in the World Cup
summary: The Paraguayan team advances in the tournament, while Japan fights but falls to the five-time world champion.

title: Japan's unemployment rate remains stable and industrial production rebounds
summary: The job offer rate falls slightly while industrial production rises 0.5% in May.

title: Japanese bonds fall on fears of delayed action by Bank of Japan
summary: Long-term bond yields rise as investors worry about monetary policy.

title: Australia sues Amazon over unsolicited advertising on Prime Video
summary: The Australian consumer authority accuses Amazon's subsidiary of violating the law by sending ads to subscribers.

title: British American Tobacco cuts 9,000 jobs in restructuring plan
summary: The tobacco company seeks to reduce costs amid declining cigarette consumption.

title: Warren Buffett suspends donation to Gates Foundation over Epstein case review
summary: The investor decides not to make the annual contribution while the foundation's ties to Jeffrey Epstein are investigated.

title: Apple accuses India of copying rivals' complaints in antitrust investigation
summary: The tech company alleges that the Indian regulator relies on baseless complaints from competitors.
